items.summary: OpenBrief is basically a GUI for yt-dlp with some AI on top — paste a link, it downloads locally, and transcription and voice generation run with local AI on your machine. items.title: AWS Weekly Roundup: AWS Local Zones in Istanbul, open-source ExtendDB, Kiro Web, and more (May 25, 2026)
items.summary: There’s something genuinely energizing about working with startups — something I’ve been doing intensely for more than two years now. Startups operate at a different frequency: the urgency is real, the constraints are tight, and the stakes are personal. Helping them navigate the challenge of proving their business model requires not just technical depth but […]
items.content: There’s something genuinely energizing about working with startups — something I’ve been doing intensely for more than two years now. Startups operate at a different frequency: the urgency is real, the constraints are tight, and the stakes are personal. Helping them navigate the challenge of proving their business model requires not just technical depth but a willingness to move fast, challenge assumptions, and make bets on the right architecture before the perfect data exists.
items.author: Daniel Abib

items.title: Ferrari Luce first look: going where combustion can’t follow
items.summary: Ferrari unveiled the Luce today in Rome, completing a three-act reveal that started with specs last October and continued with the Jony Ive-designed interior in February . The Italian automaker flew me to Rome for the launch, and I got about 30 minutes to explore the vehicle up close, though I couldn’t drive it.

items.title: What is a bike bus and why are parents loving them so much?
items.summary: For years, the school drop-off line has been one of the defining rituals of suburban life: a long queue of idling SUVs inching toward the curb while stressed parents try to make it to work on time and kids stare at their phones in the back seat. But in a growing number of communities around the world, another option is starting to catch on: the bike bus. And parents are absolutely loving it. more…

items.title: Launch HN: Chert (YC P26) – Twilio for iMessage
items.summary: Hey HN! We’re Gary and Ian, and we’re building Chert ( https://www.trychert.com/ ), an API for businesses to send, receive, and automate iMessage conversations at scale. Check out our demo: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SRdwvVxMMoI . We originally started by building products on top of iMessage because the blue bubble interface, typing indicators, and reactions made agentic conversations feel more human than ones on SMS/RCS.

items.title: Musk abandoned his own ‘solar electric economy’ to burn gas for an AI chatbot no one uses
items.summary: Elon Musk spent years telling the world that solar power was the obvious answer to Earth’s energy needs — that a small patch of desert could power the entire United States. Now, he’s burning millions of tons of fossil fuels to run an AI chatbot that has lost 60% of its downloads, selling the unused compute to a company he called “misanthropic and evil” three months ago, and pitching space-based solar panels right as SpaceX files for a $2 trillion IPO.

items.title: Exploitation of KnowledgeDeliver via ViewState Deserialization Vulnerability
items.summary: Written by: Takahiro Sugiyama, Peter Revelant, Mathew Potaczek Introduction In late 2025, Mandiant responded to a security incident involving a compromised web server running KnowledgeDeliver . KnowledgeDeliver is a Learning Management System (LMS) developed by Digital Knowledge commonly used in Japan. Mandiant identified a critical vulnerability that allowed unauthenticated Remote Code Execution (RCE).
items.author: Google Threat Intelligence Group

items.title: 2 PhaaS 2 Furious: The Evolution of Chinese-language Phishing Services
items.summary: While Russian-speaking threat actors have historically dominated the phishing-as-a-service (PhaaS) landscape, a rival ecosystem is rapidly growing within the Chinese-language underground. Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) analyzed a dozen current PhaaS offerings in the Chinese underground, all of them mature services and many likely tied intricately to the broader criminal ecosystem in that region.
items.author: Google Threat Intelligence Group

items.summary: Deal also launched the first quantum foundry company, but is there a need for it?
items.content: Last week, the US government announced $2 billion in investments in quantum computing companies, allocating $100 million each to a range of startups in exchange for equity in the companies. Those could be make-or-break investments for many companies that are likely years away from a product that could see widespread use.

items.title: Why Kubernetes policy enforcement happens too late—and what to do about it
items.summary: Kubernetes has become the backbone of modern cloud-native infrastructure. Its flexibility lets teams move fast, compose complex systems from modular components, and deploy across environments with relative ease. But that flexibility comes with a well-known cost:...
items.content: Kubernetes has become the backbone of modern cloud-native infrastructure. Its flexibility lets teams move fast, compose complex systems from modular components, and deploy across environments with relative ease. But that flexibility comes with a well-known cost: configuration complexity. Across organizations of all sizes, a surprisingly large share of reliability and security incidents don’t originate in application code.
items.author: Sajal Nigam, CNCF Community Member

items.title: Zero-Downtime migration from ingress NGINX to Envoy Gateway
items.summary: Teams running Ingress NGINX in production are increasingly evaluating migration paths as Kubernetes networking evolves toward Gateway API. For many organizations, the challenge is not just selecting a Gateway API implementation, but designing a migration strategy...
items.content: Teams running Ingress NGINX in production are increasingly evaluating migration paths as Kubernetes networking evolves toward Gateway API. For many organizations, the challenge is not just selecting a Gateway API implementation, but designing a migration strategy that minimizes operational risk during cutover.

But ask the teams running NDR that includes agentic AI capabilities and you'll hear they're actually using it to catch threats earlier, triage faster, and chase fewer false positives. The old complaint lingers in part because reputations are sticky, and because NDR has evolved

items.title: 25th May – Threat Intelligence Report
items.summary: For the latest discoveries in cyber research for the week of 25th May, please download our Threat Intelligence Bulletin. TOP ATTACKS AND BREACHES 7-Eleven, the global convenience store chain, confirmed a breach after an unauthorized access to systems used for franchisee documents. ShinyHunters claimed responsibility and said it stole more than 600,000 Salesforce records containing personal […] The post 25th May – Threat Intelligence Report appeared first on Check Point Research .
items.content: For the latest discoveries in cyber research for the week of 25th May, please download our Threat Intelligence Bulletin. TOP ATTACKS AND BREACHES 7-Eleven, the global convenience store chain, confirmed a breach after an unauthorized access to systems used for franchisee documents. ShinyHunters claimed responsibility and said it stole more than 600,000 Salesforce records containing personal and corporate information, with affected individuals offered identity protection services.
items.author: urias

items.title: Lazarus Deploys RemotePE Memory-Only RAT Against Financial and Crypto Firms
items.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a cross-platform malware called RemotePE that has been put to use by the North Korea-linked Lazarus Group in attacks targeting financial and cryptocurrency organizations. RemotePE, per NCC Group subsidiary Fox-IT, is part of a multi-stage attack chain that involves two loaders tracked as DPAPILoader and RemotePELoader. "DPAPILoader decrypts and
items.author: info@thehackernews.com (The Hacker News)

items.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
items.summary: A new coordinated cross-ecosystem software supply chain attack campaign has targeted npm, PyPI, and Crates.io to distribute credential-stealing malware. The campaign, codenamed TrapDoor, spans more than 34 malicious packages across over 384 versions. items.title: 왜 Vivado 2026.1은 무료 티어에서 Linux 지원을 중단하는가?
items.summary: Vivado 2026.1 BASIC 티어 에서 Linux 지원이 제외되는 변화가 쟁점이며, Windows에서는 가능한 입문용 설계를 Linux에서는 왜 막는지가 핵심임 공개 스레드는 2026년 5월 18일 시작됐고 23개 답변 과 1.34K 조회수 가 표시됐으며, 무료 사용자와 소...

items.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing service targeting Microsoft 365 accounts
items.summary: The FBI is warning about the Kali365 phishing-as-a-service platform (PhaaS) that is used to hijack Microsoft 365 accounts by abusing OAuth device code authentication to steal session tokens and bypass multi-factor authentication (MFA). [...]
